questions.text: Which Indian state became the first to implement the Uniform Civil Code (UCC) post-independence?

questions.explanation: Atal Setu connects Mumbai to Navi Mumbai, not Alibaug.

questions.text: At what rank does India's youth stand in the Global Mental Health Index?

questions.explanation: The assertion is true, but the reason is fabricated. The ceremony was skipped for logistical/operational reasons, not an earthquake.

questions.text: According to reports from March 2026, which country topped the global pollution index?
questions.explanation: Pakistan topped the global pollution list, while India ranked sixth.

questions.text: What is the new UPI-linked withdrawal facility under EPFO 3.0 expected by?

questions.text: Which bank launched India's first solar-powered ATM van?
questions.explanation: Tripura Gramin Bank launched 'TGB On Wheels,' India's first solar-powered mobile ATM van for remote banking services.

questions.text: What is the objective of Tripura Gramin Bank's solar ATM van initiative?
options: A. Generate revenue for the bank
B. Financial inclusion in remote areas
questions.explanation: The solar ATM van aims to provide uninterrupted banking services to remote and electricity-deficient areas, promoting financial inclusion.

questions.text: What was the combined market value of the India-EU FTA announced?
questions.explanation: The India-EU FTA generates a combined market value of USD 24 trillion, covering the 2nd and 4th largest economies.

questions.text: In which year did India and the EU first launch FTA negotiations?
questions.explanation: India and the EU first began FTA negotiations in 2007, which were suspended in 2013 and resumed in 2022 before concluding in January 2026.

options: B. Minister of Development of North Eastern Region
questions.explanation: Union Minister Jyotiraditya Scindia (Minister of Development of North Eastern Region) launched 2 bamboo projects worth Rs 82.5 crore in Guwahati.

questions.text: What was the total investment in the two bamboo projects launched in the Northeast?
questions.explanation: The two bamboo projects in the Northeast were worth Rs 82.5 crore, launched on January 26, 2026, in Guwahati.

questions.text: What is the LRASiM missile's striking speed?
questions.explanation: The Long-Range Anti-Ship Missile (LRASiM) travels at Mach 10 (approx. 3.37 km/sec), making it among the fastest missiles globally.

questions.text: What is the maximum range of India's LRASiM missile?
questions.explanation: The LRASiM has a range of 1500 km, allowing the Indian Navy to project power far beyond traditional limits.

questions.text: India became the first country to commercially produce which eco-friendly material?
questions.explanation: India became the first country to produce bio-bitumen commercially, using agricultural byproducts as an eco-friendly substitute for petroleum-based bitumen.

questions.text: What percentage of greenhouse gas reduction does bio-bitumen provide compared to conventional bitumen?
questions.explanation: Bio-bitumen reduces greenhouse gases by 60-70% compared to conventional bitumen, helping address climate change and stubble-burning pollution.

questions.text: Which organization led the development of bio-bitumen technology in India?
questions.explanation: The Council of Scientific and Industrial Research (CSIR), specifically the Indian Institute of Petroleum, developed the bio-bitumen technology.

questions.text: What is the name of Aadhaar's mascot launched in January 2026?
questions.explanation: The Unique Identification Authority of India (UIDAI) launched 'Udai' as the Aadhaar mascot to help clear citizens' doubts regarding Aadhaar-related services.

questions.text: Which state recently announced a comprehensive action plan for cheetah conservation in January 2026?

questions.text: Which Indian city launched a fully integrated 'Zero Carbon' public transport initiative in early 2026?

questions.text: What is the primary function of the INS Jatayu commissioned in March 2026?
questions.explanation: INS Jatayu was commissioned as a new naval base in Minicoy, Lakshadweep, to enhance India's operational reach in the Indian Ocean.

questions.text: Which company became the first to reach a $4 trillion market cap in 2026?
questions.explanation: Microsoft briefly crossed the historic $4 trillion market capitalization mark in early 2026.

questions.text: Which country joined the Schengen area in March 2026?
questions.explanation: Bulgaria and Romania partially joined the Schengen free-travel area, lifting air and sea border controls.

questions.text: What is 'Kalki', launched by the Indian government?
questions.explanation: Kalki is an AI-based real-time translation tool launched to break language barriers across Indian regional languages.

questions.text: Which state launched the 'Indira Gandhi Pyari Behna Samman Nidhi Yojana'?
questions.explanation: Himachal Pradesh launched this scheme to provide financial assistance to women aged 18 to 60.

questions.text: Which Indian city launched the country's first underwater metro?
questions.explanation: Kolkata inaugurated India's first underwater metro route running beneath the Hooghly River.

questions.text: Who was named the Time Person of the Year for 2025 (announced early 2026)?
questions.explanation: Sam Altman, CEO of OpenAI, was recognized for his transformative impact on artificial intelligence.

questions.text: Which Indian state inaugurated the Sela Tunnel?
questions.explanation: The Sela Tunnel, the world's longest twin-lane tunnel above 13,000 feet, was inaugurated in Arunachal Pradesh.

questions.text: Which country successfully tested the 'Avangard' hypersonic missile?
questions.explanation: Russia successfully deployed the Avangard hypersonic glide vehicle, capable of maneuvering at extreme speeds.

questions.text: What is the focus of the 'PM-SURAJ' portal?
options: B. Credit support for marginalized sections
questions.explanation: The PM-SURAJ (Pradhan Mantri Samajik Utthan and Rozgar Adharit Jankalyan) portal provides credit support to marginalized communities.

questions.text: What does the 'DigiYatra' facility offer?
questions.explanation: DigiYatra provides a decentralized mobile wallet-based identity platform for biometric boarding at Indian airports.

questions.text: Which of the following is a prominent application of Generative AI?
options: A. Sorting email into spam folders
B. Predicting housing prices using regression
C. Creating novel images from text descriptions
questions.explanation: Generative AI excels at creating new content, such as text, images, or audio, based on learned patterns.

questions.text: Phishing is a cyber attack carried out by destroying physical servers with hardware tools.
questions.explanation: Phishing is a social engineering attack where attackers deceive victims into revealing sensitive information, usually via fraudulent emails.

questions.text: The term 'Artificial Intelligence' was first coined by computer scientist John ______ at a conference in 1956.
questions.explanation: John McCarthy is recognized as one of the founding fathers of AI, having coined the term for the Dartmouth Conference.

questions.text: Which of these are practical applications of Computer Vision? (Select all correct)
options: D. Obstacle detection in self-driving cars
questions.explanation: Computer vision interprets visual data. Text translation uses NLP, and generating passwords relies on cryptographic randomization.

questions.text: Assertion: Reusable rockets have significantly decreased the cost of accessing space. Reason: Instead of burning up in the atmosphere or falling into the ocean, the first stages of these rockets land safely and can be flown again.
questions.explanation: Both statements are true. Companies like SpaceX pioneered reusable rocket technology to dramatically lower launch costs.

questions.text: Match the types of malicious software to their behavior:
pairs: Locks files until a fee is paid
questions.explanation: These are common classifications of malware based on their specific malicious functions.

questions.text: 3D printing is technically classified as what type of manufacturing?
questions.explanation: 3D printing creates objects by adding material layer by layer, hence 'additive manufacturing'.

questions.text: A botnet is a network of private computers infected with malicious software and controlled as a group without the owners' knowledge.
questions.explanation: Botnets are frequently used by hackers to launch large-scale Distributed Denial of Service (DDoS) attacks.

questions.text: The 'A' in AGI, referring to a hypothetical system that possesses human-level cognitive abilities, stands for ______.
questions.explanation: AGI stands for Artificial General Intelligence, a machine capable of understanding or learning any intellectual task a human can.

questions.text: In the context of Large Language Models (LLMs), what does an AI 'hallucination' refer to?
options: A. When the AI overheats the server hardware
B. Confidently generating false or entirely fabricated information
C. When the AI starts recognizing images in text
D. A graphical glitch on the user's screen
questions.explanation: Hallucinations occur when an AI model confidently presents incorrect, illogical, or made-up facts as the truth.

questions.text: Which Indian city hosted the World Coffee Conference in 2026?
questions.explanation: Bengaluru hosted the World Coffee Conference in early 2026, highlighting India's coffee sector.

questions.text: Where was the 14th WTO Ministerial Conference (MC14) held in March 2026?
questions.explanation: MC14 was held in Yaoundé, Cameroon, under the chairmanship of Luc Magloire Mbarga Atangana.

questions.text: The 28-year e-commerce moratorium on customs duties for digital transmissions was permanently extended at the WTO MC14.
questions.explanation: The moratorium officially lapsed, causing the 'Yaoundé Deadlock' and marking a turning point in digital trade.

questions.text: What is the primary purpose of the 'Jan Samarth Portal'?
questions.explanation: It is a national portal for Credit-Linked Government Schemes to connect citizens digitally with these programs.

questions.text: India's First Digital Census 2027 is scheduled to begin its operations in ______ 2026.
questions.explanation: The digital census is scheduled to begin in April 2026, featuring self-enumeration.

questions.text: Which country announced a ban on social media for users under 16 years old in March 2026?
questions.explanation: Indonesia banned social media for under-16 users, establishing strict new digital rules.

questions.text: Match the March 2026 discoveries/events with their locations:
questions.explanation: These events highlight significant geographical, biological, and geopolitical occurrences from March 2026.

questions.text: How many railway stations are targeted for redevelopment under the Amrit Bharat Station Scheme?
questions.explanation: The scheme aims to redevelop 1338 railway stations across India.

questions.text: Which of the following states passed the Uniform Civil Code (UCC) Bill in the first quarter of 2026? (Select all correct)
questions.explanation: Uttarakhand implemented it first, and Gujarat passed its UCC Bill in March 2026.

questions.text: Assertion: BCCI decided to skip the opening ceremony at Chinnaswamy Stadium for IPL 2026. Reason: The stadium was completely destroyed in an earthquake.
questions.explanation: The assertion is true, but the reason is fabricated. The ceremony was skipped for logistical/operational reasons, not an earthquake.

questions.text: According to reports from March 2026, which country topped the global pollution index?
questions.explanation: Pakistan topped the global pollution list, while India ranked sixth.

questions.text: The Lok Sabha passed the ______ Rights Amendment Bill in March 2026 to advance LGBTQ+ protections.
questions.explanation: The Transgender Rights Amendment Bill 2026 was passed to strengthen protections.

questions.text: What is 'Akasha300 3D', delivered to ISRO by Spacetime 4D?
questions.explanation: Akasha300 3D is a 3D printer delivered for space applications.

questions.text: The Ayush Ministry launched an in-flight yoga routine for air travellers in March 2026.
questions.explanation: This innovative initiative aims to promote wellness during air travel.

questions.text: Which hill was declared a National Geo-Heritage Site in March 2026?
questions.explanation: Kalinjar Hill was declared a National Geo-Heritage Site, recognizing its geological importance.

questions.text: How many Indian states share a land border with Pakistan?
questions.explanation: Gujarat, Rajasthan, Punjab, and Jammu & Kashmir (as a Union Territory) share a land border with Pakistan.

questions.text: Which Indian state has the longest coastline?
questions.explanation: Gujarat has the longest coastline in India, extending over 1,600 km.

questions.text: Which of the following are major rivers of India? (Select all correct)
questions.explanation: The Ganges, Yamuna, and Brahmaputra are major Indian rivers. The Amazon and Nile are not.

questions.text: Assertion: The Himalayas are called young fold mountains. Reason: They were formed recently by the collision of tectonic plates.
questions.explanation: Both statements are true. The Himalayas are geologically young and still rising due to plate tectonics.

questions.text: Match the following Indian cities to their states:
questions.explanation: These are well-known state capitals in India.

questions.text: Which is the highest peak in India?
questions.explanation: Kanchenjunga is the highest mountain peak in India.

questions.text: The ______ desert is the largest hot desert in the world.
questions.explanation: The Sahara desert in North Africa is the world's largest hot desert.

questions.text: Which Indian city is famously known as the 'Pink City'?
questions.explanation: Jaipur is famously known as the Pink City because of its distinctive building color.

questions.text: Which of these are union territories of India? (Select all correct)
questions.explanation: Ladakh, Puducherry, Chandigarh, and Delhi are union territories. Goa is a state.

questions.text: Assertion: The Thar Desert is located in Western India. Reason: It receives very high annual rainfall.
questions.explanation: The assertion is true, but the reason is false as the Thar Desert receives very little rainfall.

questions.text: Match the following continents to their largest country by area:
questions.explanation: Russia, Canada, and Brazil are the largest countries in their respective continents.

questions.text: Which of these countries is landlocked?
questions.explanation: Laos is the only landlocked country in Southeast Asia.

questions.text: The equator passes through which of these continents?
questions.explanation: The equator passes through South America, Africa, and Asia.

questions.text: The Prime Meridian passes through London.
questions.explanation: The Prime Meridian is defined as passing through the Royal Observatory in Greenwich, London.

questions.text: The ______ Ocean is the smallest ocean on Earth.
questions.explanation: The Arctic Ocean is the smallest and shallowest of the world's five oceans.

questions.text: Which mountain range separates Europe from Asia?
questions.explanation: The Ural Mountains are commonly considered the boundary between Europe and Asia.

questions.text: Which is the most populous country in the world (as of 2026)?
questions.explanation: India is now the most populous country in the world.
